question: Q3
claim: BI-TEMPORAL SYNTHESIS: unify causal+lineage only, retention stays separate. causal-edge = 2-stamp event-time (temporal-edges.ts:16-23); lineage = valid_from/valid_to + created_at, ~3/4 bi-temporal (lineage-state.ts:55-56,60); retention TTL = physical deletion w/ tier protection (memory-retention-sweep.ts:143,176,181), the OPPOSITE of edge-presence-not-deletion - folding it in is a category error. C3-B unifies causal+lineage+code_edges; retention consumes the window read-side.

question: Q3
claim: RECONCILIATION RISK: dual supersede truth - lineage superseded_by_memory_id (lineage-state.ts:54) vs causal-edge invalid_at invalidation; C3-C current-support provider must pick ONE canonical store or pay a permanent read-time join tax. Unification cost is ASYMMETRIC (lineage 3/4 there via created_at; causal-edge zero txn-time). Designate one canonical supersede writer in C3-B.
